audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with prophetic fall-of-Babylon/economic lament imagery; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Babylon fallen, come out, luxury economy, merchant lament, violence exposed.

verses.restored: After these things I saw another messenger descending from heaven, having great authority, and earth was illumined from his glory.
He cried with strong voice: Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great; she became dwelling of daimons, prison of every unclean spirit, prison of every unclean bird, prison of every unclean and hated beast.
Because all nations drank from wine of passion of her misuse, kings of earth practiced misuse with her, and merchants of earth became rich from power of her luxury.
I heard another voice from heaven saying: Come out from her, my people, so you do not participate in her sins/misalignments and do not receive from her plagues.
For her sins/misalignments were joined up to heaven, and the Divine remembered her injustices.
Give back to her as she gave, and double according to her works; in the cup she mixed, mix double for her.
As much as she glorified herself and lived luxuriously, give her that much testing and grief, because in her heart she says: I sit queen, I am not widow, and I will never see grief.
Because of this in one day her plagues will come: death, grief, famine; and she will be burned in fire, because strong is Lord Divine judging her.
Kings of earth who practiced misuse and luxury with her will weep and beat breast over her when they see smoke of her burning,
standing far off because of fear of her testing, saying: Woe, woe, the great city, Babylon the strong city, because in one hour your judgment came.
And merchants of earth weep and grieve over her, because no one buys their cargo anymore:
cargo of gold, silver, precious stone, pearls, fine linen, purple, silk, scarlet, every fragrant wood, every ivory vessel, every vessel of precious wood, bronze, iron, marble,
cinnamon, spice, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, carriages, bodies, and human souls/lives.
The fruit of your soul's desire went away from you, and all luxurious and shining things perished from you and will never be found again.
The merchants of these things, made rich from her, will stand far off because of fear of her testing, weeping and grieving,
saying: Woe, woe, the great city clothed in fine linen, purple, scarlet, gilded in gold, precious stone, and pearl,
because in one hour such wealth was made desolate. Every shipmaster, everyone sailing to a place, sailors, and all working the sea stood far off,
and cried seeing smoke of her burning, saying: What is like the great city?

They cast dust upon their heads and cried, weeping and grieving, saying: Woe, woe, the great city, in which all having ships in sea became rich from her wealth, because in one hour she was made desolate.
Rejoice over her, heaven, and holy ones and apostles and prophets, because the Divine judged your judgment from her.
A strong messenger lifted a stone like great millstone and cast into sea, saying: Thus with force Babylon the great city will be thrown down and never found again.

Sound of harpists, musicians, flute-players, trumpeters will never be heard in you again; every artisan of every craft will never be found in you again; sound of millstone will never be heard in you again.

Light of lamp will never shine in you again; voice of bridegroom and bride will never be heard in you again, because your merchants were great ones of earth, because by your sorcery all nations were misled.
And in her was found blood/life of prophets and holy ones and of all slain upon earth.

translation_notes: Babylon's cargo list climaxes in bodies and human souls/lives, exposing slavery and commodification.
Come out from her is anti-complicity with exploitative empire economy.
Sorcery can signal deceptive luxury/imperial enchantment, not random accusation.
safety_note: Do not use Babylon imagery to attack modern cities or women. This is anti-empire/economic exploitation critique, especially commodification of human lives.
